vocabularies.definition: The number 1.
vocabularies.example:
I have one apple.
There is one book.
One and one is two.

vocabularies.definition: The number 2.
vocabularies.example:
I have two hands.
Two eyes and two ears.
There are two cats.

vocabularies.definition: The number 3.
vocabularies.example:
The three little pigs.
Three birds are flying.
I have three pencils.

vocabularies.definition: The number 4.
vocabularies.example:
A car has four wheels.
Four seasons in a year.
I have four books.

vocabularies.definition: The number 5.
vocabularies.example:
High five!
I have five fingers.
Five ducks in a pond.

vocabularies.definition: The number 6.
vocabularies.example:
A ladybug has six legs.
I am six years old.
Six eggs in the box.

vocabularies.definition: The number 7.
vocabularies.example:
Seven days in a week.
The seven dwarfs.
I have seven apples.

vocabularies.definition: The number 8.
vocabularies.example:
An octopus has eight legs.
eight o'clock.
I have eight pencils.

vocabularies.definition: The number 9.
vocabularies.example:
Nine comes after eight.
I have nine toy cars.
Nine birds on the tree.

vocabularies.definition: The number 10.
vocabularies.example:
I have ten fingers.
Ten little Indians.
Ten toes on my feet.

vocabularies.definition: The number 11.
vocabularies.example:
I am eleven years old.
There are eleven apples.
Eleven comes after ten.

vocabularies.definition: The number 12.
vocabularies.example:
Twelve months in a year.
Twelve eggs make a dozen.
It is twelve o'clock.

vocabularies.definition: The color of an apple or a tomato.
vocabularies.example:
An apple is red.
A red bus.
I like the color red.

vocabularies.definition: The color of the sky or the sea.
vocabularies.example:
The sky is blue.
Blue sea.
I have a blue pen.

vocabularies.definition: The color of grass or leaves.
vocabularies.example:
Grass is green.
A green leaf.
I like green grapes.

vocabularies.definition: The color of a lemon or the sun.
vocabularies.example:
A yellow sun.
Yellow bananas.
I have a yellow shirt.

vocabularies.definition: A color between red and yellow; also a fruit.
vocabularies.example:
An orange is orange.
Orange juice.
A bright orange fish.

vocabularies.definition: A pale red color, like many flowers.
vocabularies.example:
A pink flower.
Pink cheeks.
I have a pink dress.

vocabularies.definition: A color between red and blue.
vocabularies.example:
I have a purple ball.
My favorite color is purple.
Look at that purple flower.

vocabularies.definition: The color of earth or chocolate.
vocabularies.example:
A brown bear.
Brown eyes.
I have a brown bag.

vocabularies.definition: The color of coal or the night sky.
vocabularies.example:
A black cat.
Black shoes.
The night is black.

vocabularies.definition: The color of milk or snow.
vocabularies.example:
White snow.
A white rabbit.
Milk is white.

grammars.description: Hỏi về số lượng.
grammars.example:
How many apples? 
Two apples.

grammars.description: Hỏi về màu sắc.
grammars.example:
What color is the car? 
It is red.

vocabularies.definition: The part of your body that has your eyes, ears, nose, and mouth.
vocabularies.example:
Touch your head.
Move your head.
I have one head.

vocabularies.definition: The thin things that grow on your head.
vocabularies.example:
She has long hair.
My hair is black.
Wash your hair.

vocabularies.definition: The front part of your head with your eyes, nose, and mouth.
vocabularies.example:
Wash your face.
A happy face.
A beautiful face.

vocabularies.definition: The part of your face that you see with.
vocabularies.example:
Close your eyes.
I have blue eyes.
Open your eyes.

vocabularies.definition: The part of your head that you hear with.
vocabularies.example:
Listen with your ears.
I have two ears.
A rabbit has long ears.

vocabularies.definition: The part of your face that you smell with.
vocabularies.example:
I smell with my nose.
Touch your nose.
A big nose.

vocabularies.definition: The part of your face that you eat and speak with.
vocabularies.example:
Open your mouth.
I eat with my mouth.
A fish has a small mouth.

vocabularies.definition: The part of your body where your arm joins your neck.
vocabularies.example:
Touch your shoulders.
My bag is on my shoulder.
I have two shoulders.

vocabularies.definition: The long part of your body between your shoulder and your hand.
vocabularies.example:
Wave your arms.
I have two arms.
Long arms.

vocabularies.definition: The part of your body at the end of your arm with fingers.
vocabularies.example:
Wash your hands.
Clap your hands.
I have two hands.

vocabularies.definition: One of the five long parts on your hand.
vocabularies.example:
Point with your finger.
Ten fingers.
I have a ring on my finger.

vocabularies.definition: The long part of your body that you use for standing and walking.
vocabularies.example:
I run with my legs.
Long legs.
I have two legs.

vocabularies.definition: The part of your body at the end of your leg that you stand on.
vocabularies.example:
Put your foot in the shoe.
Left foot.
Kick with your foot.

vocabularies.definition: One of the five small parts on your foot.
vocabularies.example:
Ten toes.
Wiggle your toes.
Touch your toes.

grammars.description: Giới thiệu bộ phận cơ thể.
grammars.example:
This is my nose.
These are my eyes.

vocabularies.definition: A group of people who are related to each other, like a mother and father.
vocabularies.example:
I love my family.
A big family.
Family is important.

vocabularies.definition: A female parent.
vocabularies.example:
My mother is beautiful.
I help my mother.
She is a kind mother.

vocabularies.definition: A male parent.
vocabularies.example:
My father is tall.
I play with my father.
He is a great father.

vocabularies.definition: A girl or woman who has the same parents as you.
vocabularies.example:
I have two sisters.
My sister is young.
I play with my sister.

vocabularies.definition: A boy or man who has the same parents as you.
vocabularies.example:
My brother is big.
I have one brother.
He is my little brother.

vocabularies.definition: The mother of your mother or father.
vocabularies.example:
I love my grandmother.
My grandmother is kind.
She is my father's mother.

vocabularies.definition: The father of your mother or father.
vocabularies.example:
My grandfather is old.
He tells stories.
I visit my grandfather.

vocabularies.definition: A very young child.
vocabularies.example:
The baby is sleeping.
A cute baby.
I love the baby.

vocabularies.definition: The child of your aunt or uncle.
vocabularies.example:
I play with my cousin.
He is my cousin.
My cousin lives in a big house.

grammars.description: Hỏi về danh tính.
grammars.example:
Who is this? 
This is my brother.

vocabularies.definition: A small animal with soft fur and a long tail that people keep as a pet.
vocabularies.example:
The cat is on the mat.
I have a black cat.
The cat says meow.

vocabularies.definition: An animal that people keep as a pet or for work, often called man's best friend.
vocabularies.example:
I have a big dog.
The dog is brown.
The dog says woof.

vocabularies.definition: An animal that has feathers and wings and can usually fly.
vocabularies.example:
The bird is in the tree.
A beautiful blue bird.
Birds can fly.

vocabularies.definition: A small animal with long ears and a short tail.
vocabularies.example:
A small white rabbit.
The rabbit likes carrots.
The rabbit is hopping.

vocabularies.definition: A very small animal with a long tail and small ears.
vocabularies.example:
A little grey mouse.
The mouse likes cheese.
A mouse has a long tail.

vocabularies.definition: A young or baby cat.
vocabularies.example:
A cute kitten.
The kitten is playing.
I have a small kitten.

vocabularies.definition: A young or baby dog.
vocabularies.example:
A playful puppy.
The puppy is brown.
I love my puppy.

vocabularies.definition: A small animal with short ears and no tail, often kept as a pet.
vocabularies.example:
I have a guinea pig.
A small guinea pig.
My guinea pig is soft.

vocabularies.definition: A small animal like a mouse with no tail that keeps food in its cheeks.
vocabularies.example:
A little hamster.
The hamster is in its cage.
I have a hamster.

vocabularies.definition: A small orange or red fish that people keep in a bowl or tank.
vocabularies.example:
A shiny goldfish.
The goldfish swims in the bowl.
I have a goldfish.

grammars.description: Sở hữu vật nuôi.
grammars.example:
I have got a cat.
She has got a rabbit.

vocabularies.definition: A large farm animal that is kept for its milk or meat.
vocabularies.example:
The cow is big.
A cow gives milk.
The cow says moo.

vocabularies.definition: A large animal that people ride or use for pulling heavy things.
vocabularies.example:
I can ride a horse.
A brown horse.
A fast horse.

vocabularies.definition: A common water bird with a flat beak and short legs.
vocabularies.example:
The duck swims in the pond.
A yellow duck.
The duck says quack.

vocabularies.definition: A common farm bird that is kept for its eggs or meat.
vocabularies.example:
A chicken has feathers.
Chickens lay eggs.
A little chicken.

vocabularies.definition: A farm animal with thick wool that is kept for its wool or meat.
vocabularies.example:
A white sheep.
Sheep eat grass.
A soft sheep.

vocabularies.definition: A pink or black farm animal with a flat nose and a short curly tail.
vocabularies.example:
A pink pig.
The pig lives on the farm.
A big pig.

vocabularies.definition: An animal with horns that lives on farms or in the mountains.
vocabularies.example:
A goat has horns.
The goat eats grass.
A small goat.

vocabularies.definition: An animal like a small horse with long ears.
vocabularies.example:
A grey donkey.
The donkey is strong.
A donkey says eeyore.

grammars.description: Tiếng kêu của động vật.
grammars.example:
The cow says moo.
The duck says quack.

vocabularies.definition: A large wild cat that lives in Africa and has a mane.
vocabularies.example:
The lion is the king.
A big lion.
A lion is strong.

vocabularies.definition: A large wild cat with orange and black stripes.
vocabularies.example:
A tiger has stripes.
The tiger is fast.
A big tiger.

vocabularies.definition: A very large grey animal with a long trunk and big ears.
vocabularies.example:
An elephant is big.
An elephant has a trunk.
A grey elephant.

vocabularies.definition: An animal with a long tail that lives in trees.
vocabularies.example:
A monkey likes bananas.
The monkey is funny.
A monkey can climb.

vocabularies.definition: A very tall African animal with a long neck and spots.
vocabularies.example:
A giraffe is tall.
A giraffe has a long neck.
A yellow giraffe.

vocabularies.definition: A large, heavy African animal that lives in or near rivers.
vocabularies.example:
A big hippo.
The hippo lives in the water.
A grey hippo.

vocabularies.definition: A large reptile with hard skin and big teeth that lives in water.
vocabularies.example:
A long crocodile.
The crocodile has big teeth.
A green crocodile.

vocabularies.definition: A long thin animal with no legs.
vocabularies.example:
A long snake.
A snake has no legs.
A green snake.

vocabularies.definition: A wild animal that looks like a horse with black and white stripes.
vocabularies.example:
A zebra is black and white.
A zebra looks like a horse.
A striped zebra.

grammars.description: Miêu tả đặc điểm động vật.
grammars.example:
The elephant is big.
The giraffe is tall.

vocabularies.definition: A building that people live in.
vocabularies.example:
I live in a big house.
My house is pink.
I like my house.

vocabularies.definition: A set of rooms for living in, usually on one floor of a building.
vocabularies.example:
We live in a small flat.
A flat in the city.
My flat is on the third floor.

vocabularies.definition: A room where people sleep.
vocabularies.example:
I sleep in my bedroom.
A clean bedroom.
My bedroom has a bed.

vocabularies.definition: A room with a bath or shower and a toilet.
vocabularies.example:
I wash in the bathroom.
A big bathroom.
The bathroom is clean.

vocabularies.definition: The room where food is prepared and cooked.
vocabularies.example:
Cook in the kitchen.
My kitchen is nice.
Breakfast in the kitchen.

vocabularies.definition: The main room in a house where people sit and relax.
vocabularies.example:
Watch TV in the living room.
A large living room.
Sit in the living room.

vocabularies.definition: A room where people eat their meals.
vocabularies.example:
We eat in the dining room.
A nice dining room.
Table in the dining room.

vocabularies.definition: An area of land next to a house where plants are grown.
vocabularies.example:
Flowers in the garden.
Play in the garden.
A green garden.

vocabularies.definition: The part of a room or building that opens and closes.
vocabularies.example:
Open the door.
A white door.
Close the door.

vocabularies.definition: A space in a wall that is filled with glass to let in light.
vocabularies.example:
Look out the window.
A clean window.
Close the window.

vocabularies.definition: One of the sides of a room or building.
vocabularies.example:
Paint the wall.
A white wall.
Clock on the wall.

vocabularies.definition: The surface that you walk on in a room.
vocabularies.example:
Sit on the floor.
A clean floor.
Wooden floor.

grammars.description: Hỏi vị trí.
grammars.example: Where is the cat? In the kitchen.

vocabularies.definition: A piece of furniture with a flat top and legs.
vocabularies.example:
Sit at the table.
A big table.
Put it on the table.

vocabularies.definition: A piece of furniture for one person to sit on.
vocabularies.example:
Sit on the chair.
A red chair.
A small chair.

vocabularies.definition: A type of table that you sit at to write or work.
vocabularies.example:
I study at my desk.
A clean desk.
My desk is in my room.

vocabularies.definition: A piece of furniture that you sleep on.
vocabularies.example:
Go to bed.
A soft bed.
I have a big bed.

vocabularies.definition: A piece of furniture with shelves for keeping books on.
vocabularies.example:
Books in the bookcase.
A wooden bookcase.
My bookcase is full.

vocabularies.definition: A large tall cupboard where you can hang your clothes.
vocabularies.example:
Clothes in the wardrobe.
A big wardrobe.
Close the wardrobe.

vocabularies.definition: A long comfortable seat for more than one person.
vocabularies.example:
Sit on the sofa.
A comfortable sofa.
A green sofa.

vocabularies.definition: A comfortable chair with sides for your arms to rest on.
vocabularies.example:
Sit in the armchair.
A big armchair.
A soft armchair.

vocabularies.definition: A piece of glass that you can see yourself in.
vocabularies.example:
Look in the mirror.
A clean mirror.
A large mirror.

vocabularies.definition: An object that shows what time it is.
vocabularies.example:
Look at the clock.
A round clock.
What time is it on the clock?

vocabularies.definition: An object that produces light.
vocabularies.example:
Turn on the lamp.
A bright lamp.
A desk lamp.

vocabularies.definition: An electronic machine that can store and find information.
vocabularies.example:
I use a computer.
A fast computer.
Play games on the computer.

grammars.description: Giới từ chỉ vị trí.
grammars.example:
The lamp is on the table.
The rug is under the chair.

vocabularies.definition: A place where children go to learn.
vocabularies.example:
I go to school.
My school is big.
I like my school.

vocabularies.definition: A room in a school where students have lessons.
vocabularies.example:
A bright classroom.
Sit in the classroom.
Our classroom is on the second floor.

vocabularies.definition: A person whose job is to teach students.
vocabularies.example:
My teacher is kind.
Listen to the teacher.
A great teacher.

vocabularies.definition: A person who is learning at a school or college.
vocabularies.example:
I am a student.
Many students in the school.
A good student.

vocabularies.definition: A set of pages with writing or pictures inside a cover.
vocabularies.example:
Read a book.
A fun book.
I have many books.

vocabularies.definition: A book with empty pages that you use for writing in.
vocabularies.example:
Write in the notebook.
A clean notebook.
My notebook is full.

vocabularies.definition: An object that you use for writing with ink.
vocabularies.example:
A blue pen.
Write with a pen.
I have a pen.

vocabularies.definition: An object that you use for writing or drawing, usually made of wood with a black center.
vocabularies.example:
A long pencil.
Draw with a pencil.
A sharp pencil.

vocabularies.definition: A small piece of rubber used for removing pencil marks.
vocabularies.example:
Use an eraser.
A small eraser.
A white eraser.

vocabularies.definition: A flat piece of plastic or wood used for measuring things or drawing straight lines.
vocabularies.example:
A long ruler.
Measure with a ruler.
A plastic ruler.

vocabularies.definition: A container made of cloth or plastic used for carrying things.
vocabularies.example:
A school bag.
Put it in your bag.
My bag is heavy.

vocabularies.definition: A large flat surface in a classroom that a teacher writes on.
vocabularies.example:
Write on the board.
A white board.
Look at the board.

grammars.description: Hỏi về đồ vật trong cặp.
grammars.example:
What's in your bag? 
A book and a pen.

vocabularies.definition: Something that people and animals eat to stay alive.
vocabularies.example:
I like food.
Yummy food.
Healthy food.

vocabularies.definition: A common food made from flour and water, then baked.
vocabularies.example:
Eat some bread.
A loaf of bread.
White bread.

vocabularies.definition: A sweet food made from flour, sugar, and eggs.
vocabularies.example:
A birthday cake.
A chocolate cake.
I like cake.

vocabularies.definition: A sweet brown food made from cacao beans.
vocabularies.example:
I love chocolate.
A bar of chocolate.
Dark chocolate.

vocabularies.definition: A round object produced by a bird that is often used as food.
vocabularies.example:
A fried egg.
A chicken egg.
I have an egg for breakfast.

vocabularies.definition: The part of an animal used as food.
vocabularies.example:
Eat some meat.
A piece of meat.
Red meat.

vocabularies.definition: Small white or brown grains from a plant, used as food.
vocabularies.example:
Eat rice.
A bowl of rice.
White rice.

vocabularies.definition: A long thin piece of food made from flour and water.
vocabularies.example:
Eat noodles.
I like noodles.
Hot noodles.

vocabularies.definition: An Italian food made of flat bread with tomatoes and cheese on top.
vocabularies.example:
Eat pizza.
A big pizza.
I love pizza.

vocabularies.definition: A flat round piece of meat served inside a round bread roll.
vocabularies.example:
A chicken burger.
Eat a burger.
A tasty burger.

vocabularies.definition: Long thin pieces of potato that have been fried.
vocabularies.example:
I like fries.
French fries.
Salty fries.

vocabularies.definition: A very cold sweet food made from milk.
vocabularies.example:
Eat ice cream.
A cold ice cream.
Vanilla ice cream.

grammars.description: Nói về sở thích ăn uống.
grammars.example:
I like cake.
I don't like meat.

vocabularies.definition: The sweet part of a plant that you can eat.
vocabularies.example:
I like fruit.
Eat fresh fruit.
What is your favorite fruit?

vocabularies.definition: A hard round fruit that is green or red.
vocabularies.example:
A red apple.
Eat an apple.
I have an apple in my bag.

vocabularies.definition: A long yellow fruit with soft white inside.
vocabularies.example:
A yellow banana.
Monkey likes bananas.
Eat a banana.

vocabularies.definition: A round fruit with a thick orange skin and a lot of juice.
vocabularies.example:
An orange is orange.
I like orange juice.
A sweet orange.

vocabularies.definition: A small green or purple fruit that grows in groups.
vocabularies.example:
Green grapes.
I eat grapes.
A bunch of grapes.

vocabularies.definition: A sweet fruit that is narrow at the top and wide at the bottom.
vocabularies.example:
A green pear.
Eat a pear.
A sweet pear.

vocabularies.definition: A large fruit with thick yellow skin and sharp leaves on top.
vocabularies.example:
A big pineapple.
I like pineapple.
A yellow pineapple.

vocabularies.definition: A very large green fruit that is red and sweet inside.
vocabularies.example:
A big watermelon.
Watermelon is sweet.
Red watermelon.

vocabularies.definition: A sweet tropical fruit with yellow or red skin and a large seed.
vocabularies.example:
A yellow mango.
I like mango.
A sweet mango.

vocabularies.definition: A small juicy red fruit with seeds on the outside.
vocabularies.example:
A red strawberry.
I like strawberries.
A small strawberry.

vocabularies.definition: A sour yellow fruit.
vocabularies.example:
A yellow lemon.
Lemonade is made from lemons.
A sour lemon.

vocabularies.definition: A small sour green fruit.
vocabularies.example:
A green lime.
Add some lime juice.
A sour line.

vocabularies.definition: A large nut with a hard brown shell and white inside.
vocabularies.example:
A big coconut.
Coconut water.
A brown coconut.

vocabularies.definition: A plant that is used for food, like a potato or carrot.
vocabularies.example:
Eat your vegetables.
Green vegetables.
Vegetables are healthy.

vocabularies.definition: A common white vegetable that grows under the ground.
vocabularies.example:
I like potatoes.
Baked potato.
A big potato.

vocabularies.definition: A soft red fruit that is often used as a vegetable.
vocabularies.example:
A red tomato.
Tomato soup.
I like tomatoes.

vocabularies.definition: A long orange vegetable that grows under the ground.
vocabularies.example:
An orange carrot.
Rabbit likes carrots.
Eat a carrot.

vocabularies.definition: A round white or red vegetable with a strong smell.
vocabularies.example:
A white onion.
Chop the onion.
An onion is round.

vocabularies.definition: A small round green seed that grows in a pod and is eaten as a vegetable.
vocabularies.example:
Green peas.
I eat peas.
A small pea.

vocabularies.definition: A seed or seed case of some plants that is used as food.
vocabularies.example:
Green beans.
I like beans.
A long bean.

vocabularies.definition: A tall plant with yellow grains that are eaten as a vegetable.
vocabularies.example:
Yellow corn.
Sweet corn.
I eat corn.

grammars.description: Dùng some/any với danh từ không đếm được và số nhiều.
grammars.example:
I have some apples.
Do you have any grapes?

vocabularies.definition: A liquid that you swallow.
vocabularies.example:
I want a drink.
Cold drink.
What is your favorite drink?

vocabularies.definition: The clear liquid that we drink to stay alive.
vocabularies.example:
Drink some water.
Cold water.
A glass of water.

vocabularies.definition: A white liquid produced by cows.
vocabularies.example:
Drink milk.
White milk.
A glass of milk.

vocabularies.definition: The liquid that comes from fruit.
vocabularies.example:
Fruit juice.
I like juice.
Orange juice.

vocabularies.definition: A sweet drink made from lemons and water.
vocabularies.example:
I like lemonade.
Sweet lemonade.
A glass of lemonade.

vocabularies.definition: A hot drink made by pouring water over dried leaves.
vocabularies.example:
Drink some tea.
Hot tea.
I like tea.

grammars.description: Mời ai đó dùng đồ uống/đồ ăn.
grammars.example:
Would you like some water?
Yes, please.

vocabularies.definition: The things that you wear on your body.
vocabularies.example:
I have many clothes.
Clean clothes.
Put on your clothes.

vocabularies.definition: Something that you wear on your head.
vocabularies.example:
A sun hat.
Put on your hat.
I have a red hat.

vocabularies.definition: A type of soft hat with a part that sticks out at the front.
vocabularies.example:
A baseball cap.
I wear a cap.
A blue cap.

vocabularies.definition: A soft cotton shirt with short sleeves.
vocabularies.example:
A white t-shirt.
I wear a t-shirt.
My favorite t-shirt.

vocabularies.definition: A piece of clothing for the upper body with a collar and buttons.
vocabularies.example:
A blue shirt.
Clean shirt.
Put on your shirt.

vocabularies.definition: A warm piece of clothing with sleeves, made of wool or cotton.
vocabularies.example:
A warm sweater.
I wear a sweater.
A green sweater.

vocabularies.definition: A short light coat.
vocabularies.example:
A red jacket.
Put on your jacket.
A new jacket.

vocabularies.definition: A piece of clothing worn over other clothes to keep warm.
vocabularies.example:
A warm coat.
I wear a coat in winter.
A long coat.

vocabularies.definition: A piece of clothing for women that covers the top and hangs over the legs.
vocabularies.example:
A pink dress.
She wears a dress.
A beautiful dress.

vocabularies.definition: A piece of clothing for women that hangs from the waist.
vocabularies.example:
A short skirt.
She wears a skirt.
A blue skirt.

vocabularies.definition: A piece of clothing with two legs that covers you from the waist.
vocabularies.example:
Blue trousers.
I wear trousers.
A pair of trousers.

vocabularies.definition: Short trousers that end above the knees.
vocabularies.example:
I wear shorts in summer.
Red shorts.
A pair of shorts.

vocabularies.definition: Strong trousers made of blue denim cloth.
vocabularies.example:
I like jeans.
Blue jeans.
A pair of jeans.

vocabularies.definition: Clothes that you wear on your feet inside your shoes.
vocabularies.example:
White socks.
I wear socks.
A pair of socks.

vocabularies.definition: Things that you wear on your feet to protect them.
vocabularies.example:
Black shoes.
Put on your shoes.
A pair of shoes.

vocabularies.definition: Strong shoes that cover your feet and part of your legs.
vocabularies.example:
Rain boots.
I wear boots.
A pair of boots.

vocabularies.definition: A small bag used for carrying personal things.
vocabularies.example:
A small handbag.
My mother has a handbag.
A brown handbag.

vocabularies.definition: A small clock that you wear on your wrist.
vocabularies.example:
A new watch.
Look at my watch.
What time is it on your watch?

vocabularies.definition: Something you wear in front of your eyes to help you see better.
vocabularies.example:
I wear glasses.
A pair of glasses.
Where are my glasses?

grammars.description: Hỏi ai đó đang mặc gì.
grammars.example:
What are you wearing? 
I am wearing a t-shirt and shorts.

vocabularies.definition: A game or activity that you do for exercise or fun.
vocabularies.example:
I like sports.
My favorite sport is football.
Do you play sports?

vocabularies.definition: To do an activity for fun.
vocabularies.example:
Play football.
I play with my friends.
Let's play!

vocabularies.definition: To move quickly on your legs.
vocabularies.example:
Run fast.
I can run.
Let's run to the park.

vocabularies.definition: To move through water.
vocabularies.example:
I can swim.
Swim in the sea.
Let's go swimming.

vocabularies.definition: To push yourself into the air with your legs.
vocabularies.example:
Jump high.
A kangaroo can jump.
Let's jump!

vocabularies.definition: To hit something with your foot.
vocabularies.example:
Kick the ball.
I can kick.
Kick it hard.

vocabularies.definition: To touch something suddenly and with force.
vocabularies.example:
Hit the ball.
Don't hit your friend.
Hit the drum.

vocabularies.definition: To stop and hold something that is moving through the air.
vocabularies.example:
Catch the ball.
I can catch.
Catch the bus.

vocabularies.definition: To send something through the air with your hand.
vocabularies.example:
Throw the ball.
I can throw.
Don't throw stones.

vocabularies.definition: To travel on a bicycle.
vocabularies.example:
I can ride a bike.
A new bike.
Let's ride our bikes.

vocabularies.definition: A game where you kick a ball into a goal.
vocabularies.example:
Play football.
I like football.
A football match.

vocabularies.definition: A game where you throw a ball through a net.
vocabularies.example:
Play basketball.
A big basketball.
I am good at basketball.

vocabularies.definition: A game played with rackets and a ball on a court.
vocabularies.example:
Play tennis.
A tennis ball.
I like tennis.

vocabularies.definition: A sport played with rackets and a shuttlecock.
vocabularies.example:
Play badminton.
A badminton racket.
I like badminton.

vocabularies.definition: To move your body to music.
vocabularies.example:
I can dance.
Dance to the music.
Let's dance!

vocabularies.definition: To make musical sounds with your voice.
vocabularies.example:
I can sing.
Sing a song.
She sings beautifully.

vocabularies.definition: To make a picture with a pencil or pen.
vocabularies.example:
Draw a picture.
I can draw a cat.
Use a pencil to draw.

vocabularies.definition: To make a picture with paint.
vocabularies.example:
Paint a house.
I like painting.
Use blue paint.

vocabularies.definition: Sounds that are made by voices or instruments.
vocabularies.example:
Listen to music.
I like music.
Play some music.

grammars.description: Nói về khả năng làm gì đó.
grammars.example:
I can swim.
I can't fly.

vocabularies.definition: An object that children play with.
vocabularies.example:
I have many toys.
A toy box.
My favorite toy.

vocabularies.definition: A toy that looks like a small person.
vocabularies.example:
A beautiful doll.
Play with a doll.
She has a doll.

vocabularies.definition: A round object used in games.
vocabularies.example:
A red ball.
Kick the ball.
Throw the ball.

vocabularies.definition: A machine that can move and do work like a person.
vocabularies.example:
A cool robot.
I have a robot.
My robot can walk.

vocabularies.definition: A soft toy that looks like a bear.
vocabularies.example:
A soft teddy bear.
I sleep with my teddy bear.
A cute teddy bear.

vocabularies.definition: A thin rubber bag filled with air.
vocabularies.example:
A red balloon.
A big balloon.
The balloon is flying.

vocabularies.definition: A toy made of paper or cloth that flies in the wind on a string.
vocabularies.example:
Fly a kite.
A colorful kite.
My kite is high in the sky.

vocabularies.definition: An activity done for fun with rules.
vocabularies.example:
Play a game.
A fun game.
Computer game.

vocabularies.definition: A long vehicle that travels on tracks.
vocabularies.example:
A toy train.
The train goes choo-choo.
I have a train set.

vocabularies.definition: A vehicle with wings that flies in the sky.
vocabularies.example:
A toy plane.
The plane can fly.
A white plane.

grammars.description: Nói về trò chơi yêu thích.
grammars.example:
I like playing with my robot.
She likes playing with her doll.

vocabularies.definition: A vehicle with four wheels for carrying a few people.
vocabularies.example:
A red car.
My father has a car.
Drive a car.

vocabularies.definition: A large vehicle that carries many people.
vocabularies.example:
Go to school by bus.
A yellow bus.
Wait for the bus.

vocabularies.definition: A vehicle with two wheels that you move with your legs.
vocabularies.example:
I have a bike.
Ride a bike.
A new bike.

vocabularies.definition: A vehicle with two wheels and an engine.
vocabularies.example:
A fast motorbike.
Ride a motorbike.
A blue motorbike.

vocabularies.definition: A vehicle used for traveling on water.
vocabularies.example:
A boat on the sea.
Row a boat.
A small boat.

vocabularies.definition: A large boat for traveling across the sea.
vocabularies.example:
A big ship.
A ship on the ocean.
Watch the ship.

vocabularies.definition: A large vehicle for carrying heavy things.
vocabularies.example:
A big truck.
A red truck.
A truck carries things.

vocabularies.definition: A vehicle that flies and has large blades on top that turn.
vocabularies.example:
A helicopter in the sky.
A noisy helicopter.
Watch the helicopter.

vocabularies.definition: A vehicle that is used for carrying things or groups of people.
vocabularies.example:
A white van.
A delivery van.
A big van.

grammars.description: Hỏi về phương tiện di chuyển.
grammars.example:
How do you go to school? By bus.
How do you go to the park? By bike.

vocabularies.definition: A public garden where people can walk and play.
vocabularies.example:
Play in the park.
A big park.
Go to the park.

vocabularies.definition: A road in a city or town with buildings along it.
vocabularies.example:
Walk on the street.
A busy street.
Cross the street.

vocabularies.definition: An area of sand next to the sea.
vocabularies.example:
Go to the beach.
Swim at the beach.
A beautiful beach.

vocabularies.definition: A place where wild animals are kept so people can look at them.
vocabularies.example:
Visit the zoo.
Watch animals at the zoo.
A big zoo.

vocabularies.definition: A building where you buy things.
vocabularies.example:
Go to the shop.
A toy shop.
Buy things at the shop.

vocabularies.definition: A place where people buy and sell food and other things.
vocabularies.example:
Go to the market.
Buy fruit at the market.
A busy market.

vocabularies.definition: A place where sick people are treated by doctors.
vocabularies.example:
Doctors work in the hospital.
A big hospital.
Go to the hospital.

vocabularies.definition: A large and important town.
vocabularies.example:
Live in the city.
A big city.
Many cars in the city.

vocabularies.definition: The land outside of towns and cities.
vocabularies.example:
Live in the countryside.
A quiet countryside.
Green fields in the countryside.

grammars.description: Hỏi ai đó đang đi đâu.
grammars.example:
Where are you going? 
I am going to the park.

vocabularies.definition: Large in size.
vocabularies.example:
A big elephant.
A big house.
My dog is big.

vocabularies.definition: Little in size.
vocabularies.example:
A small mouse.
A small cat.
My toy is small.

vocabularies.definition: Feeling pleased or glad.
vocabularies.example:
A happy face.
I am happy.
A happy boy.

vocabularies.definition: Feeling unhappy.
vocabularies.example:
A sad face.
I am sad.
Don't be sad.

vocabularies.definition: Having a high temperature.
vocabularies.example:
Hot weather.
Hot tea.
I am hot.

vocabularies.definition: Having a low temperature.
vocabularies.example:
Cold water.
Cold weather.
I am cold.

vocabularies.definition: Measuring a large distance from end to end.
vocabularies.example:
A long snake.
Long hair.
A long pencil.

vocabularies.definition: Not long, or not tall.
vocabularies.example:
A short pencil.
A short boy.
Short hair.

vocabularies.definition: Not old; made recently.
vocabularies.example:
A new toy.
A new car.
I have new shoes.

vocabularies.definition: Having existed for a long time.
vocabularies.example:
An old book.
An old man.
My shoes are old.

vocabularies.definition: Of a high quality or pleasant.
vocabularies.example:
A good boy.
A good book.
You are good.

vocabularies.definition: Not good; unpleasant.
vocabularies.example:
A bad dream.
A bad boy.
That is bad.

vocabularies.definition: Not dirty.
vocabularies.example:
Clean hands.
A clean room.
I am clean.

vocabularies.definition: Not clean.
vocabularies.example:
Dirty hands.
A dirty face.
My shoes are dirty.

grammars.description: Dùng tính từ để miêu tả.
grammars.example:
The elephant is big.
The mouse is small.

vocabularies.definition: To move on your legs at a regular speed.
vocabularies.example:
Walk to school.
I can walk.
Let's walk.

vocabularies.definition: To rest on your bottom with your body upright.
vocabularies.example:
Sit down.
Sit on the chair.
I am sitting.

vocabularies.definition: To be on your feet in an upright position.
vocabularies.example:
Stand up.
Stand on the floor.
I am standing.

vocabularies.definition: To use your eyes to see.
vocabularies.example:
Look at the bird.
Look at me.
Look out the window.

vocabularies.definition: To give attention to a sound.
vocabularies.example:
Listen to the music.
Listen to the teacher.
I am listening.

vocabularies.definition: To say words with your voice.
vocabularies.example:
Speak English.
Speak slowly.
I can speak.

vocabularies.definition: To look at and understand words.
vocabularies.example:
Read a book.
I can read.
I am reading.

vocabularies.definition: To make letters or words with a pen or pencil.
vocabularies.example:
Write your name.
Write in the notebook.
I am writing.

vocabularies.definition: To use a crayon or paint to add color to a picture.
vocabularies.example:
Color the picture.
I like coloring.
Use red to color.

vocabularies.definition: To make a picture with a pencil or pen.
vocabularies.example:
Draw a dog.
I can draw.
I am drawing.

vocabularies.definition: To show something by holding out your finger towards it.
vocabularies.example:
Point to the board.
Point to your nose.
Don't point.

vocabularies.definition: To move your hand from side to side to say hello or goodbye.
vocabularies.example:
Wave goodbye.
Wave your hand.
I am waving.

vocabularies.definition: To move something to a particular place.
vocabularies.example:
Put it on the table.
Put your bag down.
Put on your shoes.

vocabularies.definition: To let someone have something as a gift.
vocabularies.example:
Give me the book.
Give it to me.
I give you an apple.

vocabularies.definition: To move something away from a place.
vocabularies.example:
Take a pencil.
Take it.
Don't take my toy.

grammars.description: Câu mệnh lệnh.
grammars.example:
Sit down!
Stand up!
Open your book!
